title: Error Response Envelope
description: The canonical HTTP response shape for every 4xx and 5xx error emitted by any service. Aligns with Laravel's default validation-error shape (spatie/laravel-data + FormRequest fallback) so downstream clients can unwrap errors uniformly.
$comment: JSON Schema draft 2020-12. Seam 3 (Data). Producer: every HTTP surface. Consumer: every HTTP client. The HTTP status code is the single source of truth for the class of error; the body carries the display copy + optional field-level validation details. Body-level 'success': false / 'status' flags are BANNED — they duplicate the HTTP status.

message.description: User-facing error message. Rendered as a toast or an inline alert on the frontend. Never contains a stack trace, request id, or internal token. For 5xx errors this is a generic 'Something went wrong' — actionable details live in server logs.

errors.description: Optional field-level validation errors. Present only on 422 Unprocessable Entity responses; absent on other status codes. Keys are the field paths (dot-separated for nested fields); values are arrays of translated error strings.

correlation_id.description: Optional X-Correlation-Id echo. Present when the server catches the error mid-request; the frontend surfaces it in support tickets so ops can grep logs.
